evals.prompt: I'm launching a craft brewery in Bristol and need a website with e-commerce for merch, a taproom booking system, and a blog for beer culture content. I've got a logo and some brand colours but nothing else built yet. I want to set up an agent company for this project. I'm thinking maybe a design agent, a content agent, and a web dev agent but I'm not sure if that's right.
evals.expected_output: A complete .claude/agents/ directory with one subagent per role, plus COMPANY.md at project root. Should challenge the 3-agent suggestion and address SEO/visibility.
expectations: COMPANY.md exists at project root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter containing name, description, tools, model, and memory fields
Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger (specifies when to invoke, mentions exclusions) — not a generic bio
Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
No agent-company/ folder is created (obsolete in v2.1 — native memory replaces it)
The skill questions or challenges the user's initial 3-agent suggestion rather than blindly accepting it
An SEO or visibility concern is explicitly addressed — either as its own agent or assigned to a specific agent in the ownership map
The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before presenting the design
Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content (not placeholder text)
Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice like 'don't be vague')
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources before giving opinions, with URL citation
Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies

evals.prompt: I run a small music production studio and I want agents to help me manage the business side — client outreach, social media, invoicing, and project management. I don't need anything technical or code-related, just the business operations. Set up an agent company for this.
evals.expected_output: A complete .claude/agents/ directory for a non-technical business operations team plus COMPANY.md at project root. No web/design/tech agents.
expectations: COMPANY.md exists at project root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model, memory)
Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger — specifies when to invoke and when not to
Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
No agent-company/ folder is created
No web development, design, or technical coding agent is created — the user explicitly said non-technical
Agent analytical frameworks reference practical creative-industry or small-business methods, not generic corporate strategies
The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before presenting the design
Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content relevant to a music production studio
Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources before giving opinions, with URL citation
Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies

evals.prompt: Here's my project brief: we're building a SaaS platform for independent restaurants to manage their online presence — menus, reviews, local SEO, social media scheduling. The codebase already exists with a Next.js frontend in src/app/, Tailwind config at tailwind.config.ts, and a Supabase backend. I need an agent company that covers product strategy, frontend design, content for the marketing site, and developer experience. Maybe 6 agents? I want them properly tested and ready to go.
evals.expected_output: A complete .claude/agents/ directory with native-memory subagents and COMPANY.md at project root. Should reference real codebase paths in source-of-truth guidance and evaluate whether 6 agents is right.
expectations: COMPANY.md exists at project root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model, memory)
Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger with inclusion and exclusion criteria
Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
No agent-company/ folder is created
At least one agent references actual codebase paths like src/app/, tailwind.config.ts, or supabase/ in its body
The skill evaluates whether 6 agents is the right number rather than blindly accepting the user's suggestion
The transcript shows evidence of running a quality gate checklist before presenting the design
Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content
Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources before giving opinions, with URL citation
Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
